note: N=3 back-to-back, nice -19, single-load, BGE server resident; identical GGUF both engines

probe: down-CSR (owner-scan partials + chain replay, nsb=22 shape)

verdict: QAT-Q4_0 daily; NVFP4 ST port NOT justified (ceiling below achieved QAT 178.7); see research/gemma4-qat-vs-nvfp4.md

first_light.gates: argmax MATCH prefill==decode; chat 'Paris'; dense arm (no MoE/parallel branch)

pairing: interleaved same-session, chat-22 prompt, n=128 greedy

Driver kept behind BW24_GRAPH_GATE as a diagnostic; v4_dc/dpl16_dc twins ARE the shipped dc path's kernels.

milestone: dc-eager decode ships (device counters, device argmax, 4B/token host)
negatives: graph capture (167 vs dc-eager 191, bucket churn + alloc nodes)
gates: DC-GATE stream IDENTICAL short+depth; VERIFY-GATE K=7; chat Jupiter; spec stream identity

probe: fa v4 t_kv-threshold at the 1024 window (+ rows_smem_w twin)
verdict: NET NEGATIVE at default; machinery kept behind seams
rows_smem_w: row-0-only argmax divergence vs the tokenwise decode chain (rows 1..k match) — VERIFY-GATE caught it; root-cause pending

probe: depth fa lane switch root-cause round 2 (reg_w register windowed twin)
verdict: BLOCKED — register windowed pair diverges; v4 pair ships (bit-exact)
findings: VERIFY-GATE logit maxdiff instrumented: default v4 config = 0.000e0 at every position (bit-exact parity architecture confirmed)
triage: unwindowed rows == T1 register BIT-EXACT on the window view (contract holds)

reg_w AND smem_w produce IDENTICAL wrong outputs (~2e-2 everywhere) vs T1-register on the same keys/q/geometry — the windowed twins agree with each other, disagree with the T1 pair; v4_w@window pairs bit-exact with decode-v4
win+/-1 sweep confounded by the fa_v4_at(window) lane gate switching kernels mid-sweep
state: v4 everywhere restored; reg_w/smem_w quarantined (unreachable at defaults); depth verify 203.8, depth VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0

probe: windowed-twin divergence ROOT CAUSE + parity-law fix
verdict: ROOT-CAUSED and FIXED architecturally: nvcc compiles textually identical kernels differently (SASS: fa_decode_vec_q 2x-unrolled vs its rows_w clone — FFMA 50 vs 25, all ops ~2x; the unpinned score `part += q*k` chain then rounds apart ~1e-2). Clone-based verify parity is unsound; v4/v4_w pair was lucky codegen.
evidence: W=2 single split: diverges 6.5e-2 -> scores only
SASS op-mix 2x ratio across the board
fix: PARITY LAW: decode AND verify both launch the SAME fa_decode_vec_q_rows_w symbol in the windowed regime (decode passes t=1; eager + dc-eager + verify straddle-loop). Bitwise parity by symbol identity on every lane: VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0 at depth on v4_w, smem_w, reg_w lanes; stream 128/128 all three.

smem_w_lane.verdict: KILL — smem twin at t=1 catastrophic

conclusion: v4 lane stays the depth winner; lane-switch lever CLOSED (no gain under exact parity). Architecture no longer depends on codegen luck; rollback seam BW24_GEMMA_ROWS_W=0.

probe: hd512 rows twin for global layers (verify batch + decode t=1, parity law)
verdict: LANDED — depth spec 203.7->207.0 (+1.6%), short spec 310.4/311.2 (id prompt N=2), depth plain 155.1->153.0 (-1.3%, rows_dpl16 t=1 slower than lucky-codegen dpl16; unroll-2 probe flat, removed)
detail: spec-only nsys: decode-loop GPU 15.5ms/round == wall (GPU-bound); dpl16 per-token verify loop was 11.5% (20 launches/round); batched rows_dpl16 = 5 launches. K sweep at depth (spec-only): K=2 222.0, K=3 231.0, K=4 232.7, K=5 231.8, K=6 225.1 — plateau K=3-5.
next: verify MoE down8_fma_dev_q8_rows_g at 54% of byte wall (60.1us vs 32.5 floor) = ~5% spec lever; verify head q6_K b4_r2 786us (86% wall).

probe: down8 CSR dedup twin at nsb=22 (ranked lever 3 — 'probe once')
verdict: NEGATIVE, arm killed: depth spec 207.0 -> 189.4/189.9 (-8.5%) despite bitwise exactness (VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0). Dedup serialization (one block computing up to t members per weight group + first-claimer scan) costs more than the ~8MB/layer saved — same physics as the qwen nsb=16 negative.
detail: design was staged accs + slot-ordered chain kernel (parity-safe); code removed per flags doctrine, this row is the record.

probe: sp16 ladder re-probe under the parity law (seam BW24_FA_SP16, no code change)
verdict: NEGATIVE again: depth plain 154.5/154.9 (+~2) but depth spec 169.5/169.8 (-18%) — v4_w's fixed per-block staging (11.5KB smem prep) doubles with 64 splits x 4 verify rows. Parity clean this time (VERIFY-GATE PASS) — the collapse is pure perf, not the old parity break.

probe: down8 slot-parallel w8 rows twin (block (32,8), staged + slot-ordered chain)
verdict: NEGATIVE, killed: depth spec 204.3/203.3 vs 206.9 serial. The serial rows_g is not latency-starved at t=4 (8x warp oversubscription hurts); t=1's w8r2 win does not transfer.
note: drafter-head mystery closed: head rides mmvq_mr2 at ~150us ~= its 137MB byte wall (mr2 avg 14.8us hides it; Max 184us). Draft chain already near floor.

probe: short-ctx spec round decomposition (nsys spec-only, chat-22, K=2, 105 rounds)
verdict: ROOT OF THE MTP GAP QUANTIFIED: verify trunk b4/b4_r2 = 205 launches/round of 10-15us matvecs running at 41% of the byte wall (2.62ms/round vs 1.08 floor) — pure launch/tail latency. llama's MTP round (10.1ms vs our 11.7 at matched K=3 shape) closes exactly this class with CUDA graphs.
next: round-stream/verify-graph arc: fixed-shape verify step (t, ctx-bucket) captured once with device-counter KV lens (dc machinery exists) — est +1.5ms/round = 222->~260 short (llama MTP 253), similar class at depth.

probe: fused qkv b4 triple/pair for verify trunk (one launch per layer's projections)
verdict: FLAT, reverted: short spec 220.4/221.3 vs 222.3, depth 206.5 vs 206.9 — bitwise exact (VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0) but zero gain. REFRAME: the verify trunk's 41%-of-wall is NOT launch-gap latency (stream already saturated, launches overlap); it is per-warp DRAM latency INSIDE q4_0_mmvq_batched's walk (one 18B block load in flight per warp between dp4a chains; wq 3.25MB in 11.6us = 280GB/s).
next: the real b4 lever = memory pipelining inside the batched walk (2 blocks in flight / wider loads / cp.async prefetch) — touches the shipped b4 family, VERIFY-GATE + battery arbitrate; est up to +1.5ms/round if it reaches gate_up's 94% eff. Alternatively the verify-graph arc for the whole-round latency class.

probe: b4 batched-walk software pipeline (2 weight blocks in flight)
verdict: FLAT, reverted (short spec 219.3/220.1 vs 222.3, depth 206.5 vs 206.9; bitwise exact). Third flat/negative shot at the verify-trunk 41%-of-wall kernel (fuse-launch, dedup, prefetch) -> the bottleneck is the 18-byte q4_0 block stride forcing narrow LSU loads (u16+4xu8-class), not DRAM latency or launches.
next: STRUCTURAL fix = load-time weight repack to an aligned layout (d-array + qs-array split or 20B-padded stride) with a b4-repack twin — the rp repack infra exists (qmatvec rp arg); VERIFY-GATE + battery arbitrate. This is the remaining quantified spec lever (verify trunk 2.62 -> ~1.5ms/round, short 222 -> ~250).

probe: Q4_0 SPLIT-PLANE DECODE MIRRORS (BW24_Q4RP, default ON) — the 18B-straggle cure
verdict: LANDED, all cells move: short plain 190.3->193.9 (+1.9%), short spec 221.2->235.1 (+6.3%), depth plain 152.9->155.3 (+1.6%), depth spec 206.2->214.9 (+4.2%). N=2 each. Gates: run-gen MATCH, VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0 depth K=4, stream 128/128 short+depth.
design: A6 NVFP4 split-plane recipe applied to Q4_0: qs plane [out_f x nblk x 16B] + d plane [out_f x nblk x 2B], built device-side at load (q4_0_split_rp_build) as a MIRROR — raw GGUF bytes stay for prefill/gemm/Stage-A; the m<=8 mmvq/batched/fused dispatch prefers the mirror via _rp twins (mr2/fused2/fused3/b2/b4/b8/b4_r2/b8_r2). Scope: 26B MoE-class trunk (attn + shared FFN, 210 tensors ~0.7GB) + full drafter. 31B dense NOT mirrored (15GB mirror exceeds VRAM — the full layout swap is the follow-up).
microbench: rp_q4_probe (wq shape 2048x2816): m=1 1.34x (688->923 GB/s), m=3 1.17x, m=4 1.13x, bitwise-exact — the 18B stride costs sector overfetch + narrow LSU loads.

probe: llama MTP re-pairing post-q4rp (interleaved, llama-server --spec-type draft-mtp warm, chat water-cycle n=128)
verdict: corrected bar: llama MTP short = ~290 warm (the recorded 241.8-253 was stale) -> short spec cell 0.80x. ROOT DIFFERENCE IS ACCEPTANCE, not round cost: llama draft acceptance 0.638-0.699, mean accepted len 2.89-3.10 (adaptive draft length, --spec-draft-n-min 1 + p-min gating) vs ours 0.520 fixed-K tok/round 2.56 — same drafter, same prompt, both greedy.
lever: ADAPTIVE DRAFT LENGTH: stop the draft chain when the drafter is unconfident (llama's p-min gate). Device-async variant for our zero-sync round: (a) logit-margin proxy kernel gating subsequent draft steps via device flag, or (b) host Markov heuristic (next round's K from last round's acceptance — no sync cost). Est +15-20% spec at equal round cost (llama gets 3.1 tok/round vs our 2.56 from the same drafter).
note: our K sweep NGEN=256 solo: short K=3 266.7 K=2 263.6; depth K=3/4/5 242.4/242.5/242.0 — NGEN=128 runs read lower (231.5) — length amortization; pairing above is NGEN=128 vs n_predict=128 matched.

probe: ADAPTIVE DRAFT LENGTH (default ON; BW24_SPEC_ADAPT=0 reverts) + llama MTP bars re-paired
verdict: LANDED: depth spec paired 214.9 -> 236.7 (+10.1%); short solo 231.1 -> 239.2 (+3.5%, accept .52->.58); short paired flat (235.9). Zero-sync host policy: k_next = clamp(m+1, 1, cap). Policy sweep: floor1/cap3 best short; depth cap3-6 flat (234.8-235.2). Gates: VERIFY-GATE PASS, stream 128/128 short+depth.

standing.gap_attribution: round cost, not acceptance: depth tok/round OURS 3.88 vs THEIRS 3.71 at accept 0.85-vs-0.90, but round 16.5ms vs 12.2ms — llama CUDA-graphs the verify+draft chain. THE remaining spec lever = verify/round graph capture (dc-style device-counter KV lens, per (t, ctx-bucket)).

probe: plain bars re-paired (llama-server warm, today's build)
analysis: every red cell shares one cause: llama's CUDA-graphed step/round (depth plain their warm serve gained ~5 over the stale bar; spec round 12.2ms vs our 16.5 at equal tok/round). THE arc = graph capture rebuilt on the parity-law machinery: dc device-counter KV lens exist; prior negative (167 vs 191 short) was bucket churn + alloc nodes -> persistent scratch pool + per-(t,ctx-bucket) verify graphs. Applies to depth plain, short spec, depth spec simultaneously.

probe: graph arc step 1 LANDED (device-len rows attention) + graph baseline re-measured
step1: fa rows_{v4_w,smem_w,reg_w,dpl16} now read the causal base from an i32 device counter (base_plus convention: dc len_d/-1, verify synced counter/0 via new async i32_set_k). All gates green, perf flat — the windowed regime is now graph-replayable.
graph_baseline_today: GRAPH-GATE short: graph 163.6-168.2 vs eager 178.5-184.3 (still -8%, stream identical). Same kernels in replay -> overhead = replay machinery (~450 alloc/free nodes per captured step + per-step tiny HtoD). STEP 2 = scratch arena so captured steps stop allocating; qwen's shipped generate_graph (capture/snapshot/rollback/bucket machinery in decode.rs:516) is the working template.
note: eager pos_d = htod_i32 per step (pageable) in some paths — sweep those to i32_set_k during the arena pass.

probe: graph gate at mid-ctx (700-tok prompt)
verdict: REGRESSION FOUND in the (non-shipped) graph path: stream 19/128 MISMATCH — the capture arm (cap_bucket_max=Some) still launches the fa_decode_dc family while eager/dc-eager ride the parity-law rows symbols since step 1; the lanes diverge once globals cross fa512_min (ctx>=512). Graph perf also still -7% at wide buckets (157.9 vs eager 169.3) so bucket churn alone is not the -8% either.
fix_plan: step 3 does both: port the gemma capture arm to the device-len rows symbols (rows_w / rows_dpl16 with kvl.len_d, the point of step 1), with fa bucket keys split at win and fa512_min so windowed-ness is constant per bucket; THEN re-measure graph vs eager before deciding on the alloc-node arena (qwen graphs win WITH allocs, so nodes may not be the cost).
shipped_paths_unaffected: dc-eager serving + spec + all gates remain green (graph is gate-only until it wins).

probe: graph arc step 3: parity-law capture arm + rung buckets — gemma graphs now CORRECT at every ctx
landed: capture arm ports to the device-len rows symbols (rows_dpl16 globals >= fa512, rows_w windowed > win), pinned per-arm by (b_swa exact-key, b_glob rung-end)

fa_geom_eager taught the hd512 dpl16 vec lane (the old key bucketed hd512 by scalar 256-splits while the kernels split by the ladder -> n_splits changed WITHIN buckets = the pos-19 mismatch + partials OOB illegal-address)
power-of-2 RUNG buckets for the rows family (kernels derive splits from len_d; grid sized for rung end, excess splits exit) — depth = ONE capture total (was 17/128 toks)
combine_rows_dc gains base_plus; hd512 rows callers all share the dc combine symbol
gates: GRAPH-GATE IDENTICAL 128-256/128-256 at short/mid/window-cross/depth; VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0; run-gen MATCH; spec stream 128/128 (235.7 depth unchanged)
perf: graph vs eager: depth +3.7-5% (154.3-156.9 vs 148.9-151.3), short/mid still capture-amortization-bound (5 captures/128). graph vs SHIPPED dc-eager at depth n=256: 156.0 vs 155.5 — parity, NOT a win.
verdict: LAUNCH OVERHEAD EXONERATED as the depth-plain gap: dc-eager is already tight. The 155-vs-168.7 llama gap re-attributes to ATTENTION KERNEL EFFICIENCY at depth — our depth attention adds 1.28ms/step (rows_v4_w 26us x 25 windowed at 5.5x its 4MB traffic floor + globals ~0.45ms) vs llama's 0.41ms. Next lever = the windowed kernel itself (v4 per-block staging amortization / occupancy at t=1), freely tunable under the parity law. Spec round graphs (step 4) remain open for the spec cells.
no_arena_needed: alloc nodes fine in replay (qwen precedent held); the old 167-vs-191 negative was bucket churn all along.

probe: hd512 global split override (BW24_FA_SP512, default 16)
verdict: LANDED: depth spec 236.9 -> 250.4/250.5 (+5.7%), depth plain 155.4 -> 156.5 (N=2). Root: gemma globals n_head_kv=2 -> grid (2 x ~29 splits) = 58 blocks on 82 SMs, rows_dpl16 8x off its byte floor; sp16 doubles the split count for occupancy. Sweep: 12=243.8, 16=250.4, 24=239.4, 32=244.5/plain-146, default(ladder)=236.9. The parity law makes the partition freely tunable — every hd512 caller (decode/verify/graph) shares fa_decode_rows, so verify and decode move together (VERIFY-GATE PASS by construction).
gates: run-gen MATCH, VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0, GRAPH-GATE IDENTICAL (graph 156.6 > eager 150.8 at depth), short unchanged (193.2/239.0)
standing: depth spec 250.4 vs llama 303-305 = 0.83x; depth plain 156.5 vs 168.7 = 0.93x; short plain 1.07x margin; short spec 239 vs 290 = 0.82x

probe: windowed split override (BW24_FA_SPW, default 64)
verdict: LANDED: depth plain 156.6 -> 159.3, depth spec 251.2 -> 253.4 (N=2 both). sp sweep at win 1024: 16=spec-collapse 179.5 (v4 staging x splits x rows), 24=159.4/250.9, 32(old default)=156.6/251.2, 48=161.9/243.0 (plain-best but spec -10.7), 64=159.3/253.4 (dominates default on BOTH). t=1 occupancy prefers fewer bigger splits (staging amortization); verify t=4 tolerates 64.
gates: run-gen MATCH, VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0, GRAPH IDENTICAL (graph 158.9 > eager 153.1), short unchanged 193.1/238.5
standing: depth plain 159.3 vs llama 168.7 = 0.944x; depth spec 253.4 vs 303-305 = 0.83x; short plain 1.07x; short spec 0.82x

probe: draft-global (fa_decode hd512 T=1) split override sp16
verdict: FLAT, reverted (253.3-253.8 vs 253.4 baseline N=2) — the drafter's 3 global-attention launches/round (107.8us each) are off the critical path at these magnitudes. Doctrine: no gain = no change.
note_owner_q: gemma drafter head is NOT FR-trimmed: both trim probes (top-N-ids .52->.34, corpus-ranked gather) lost acceptance (jsonl 2026-07-10); seam BW24_GEMMA_DRAFT_RANKS stays default-off. Post-q4rp the full head reads ~25% cheaper via mr2_rp, further shrinking the trim upside.

probe: K=V-shared global attention twin (fa_decode_vec_q_rows_dpl16_kv)
verdict: LANDED, biggest single depth win of the campaign: depth plain 159.3 -> 164.5/165.0 (+3.5%), depth spec 253.4 -> 271.3/271.6 (+7.1%), N=2. dc-gate depth 167.4 (0.992x llama 168.7).
design: gemma globals have wv:=wk — the V plane (q5_1) stores the SAME VALUES as the K plane (q8_0). The twin reuses the dequantized+bf16 q8_0 key chunk as the value chunk: V plane never read (~40% less global KV traffic, half the dq ALU), and value precision RISES q5_1->q8_0 (strictly finer numeric config; battery green). Parity structural — every hd512 caller shares fa_decode_rows(kv_shared).
gates: run-gen MATCH, VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0 K=4, GRAPH IDENTICAL (graph 165.7 > eager 159.4), DC IDENTICAL (167.4), stream 128/128 depth+short; short cells unchanged (192.5/238.0)
standing: depth plain 165-167 vs llama 168.7 = 0.98-0.99x; depth spec 271.6 vs 303-305 = 0.90x; short plain 1.07x; short spec 0.82x
followup: global V plane could stop being WRITTEN/allocated entirely (halve global KV cache + append cost) — small extra win, touch append_kv + cache sizing later.

probe: INCIDENT + REVERT: K=V-shared global twin was WRONG
what_happened: the wv:=wk premise fails post-cache: cached K is k-normed + roped, cached V is neither — the kv twin fed roped keys in as values. The +3.5 plain / +7.1 spec 'win' (commits 5eace53, and the spw=48 flip c4dfb6d-followup tuned on it) is VOID.
why_gates_missed_it: THE PARITY LAW'S BLIND SPOT: VERIFY-GATE, stream agreement, DC/GRAPH gates all compare paths that SHARED the wrong symbol — bit-exact agreement on wrong math. The independent oracle is prefill-vs-decode (run-gen) whose SHORT prompt never enters the fa512 global regime; the DEPTH run-gen (long-ids) caught it: maxdiff 21.7 MISMATCH vs 4.3-4.6 MATCH healthy.
action: twin retired in dispatch (kernel body left, unreachable, comment documents), kv_shared=false everywhere, spw default back to the honestly-swept 64. Depth run-gen (long prompt) ADDED to the standing battery.
honest_standing: depth cells return to the pre-kv marks: plain ~159 / spec ~253 paired (spw64, sp512=16, N=2 clean windows). Late-day rig thermals invalidate fresh medians (131-161 swings) — re-bench cool. llama bars are thermal-dependent too (plain depth 161.8 hot vs 168.7 cool same day).
salvage: a CORRECT global-V lever still exists: store V in q8_0 instead of q5_1 (precision parity with K, no traffic saving), or a real K=V design would require caching pre-rope values — separate assessment.

probe: rows_dpl16 two-pass split rewrite (score-all-then-weight, sc[16]+p[16] registers)
verdict: NEGATIVE, killed: clean A/B post-cooldown 2p 153.9-154.4/236.7-237.8 vs online-walk 158.8-159.2/251.7-252.6 (-3% plain, -6% spec) — the extra 32 registers/thread (on top of dpl16's 32) collapses occupancy. The 30x-off-floor kernel needs a register-frugal attack: 2-key interleave (2 live score chains max) or smem-staged scores; est headroom still ~1.4ms/round.
standing_confirmed: honest depth marks re-confirmed post-thermal-recovery: plain 159, spec 252 paired (spw64/sp512-16, all gates green incl the new depth run-gen).

probe: rows_dpl16 2-key interleave (i2, default ON; BW24_FA_I2=0 reverts)
verdict: LANDED: depth plain 158.8 -> 163.4/163.5 (+2.9%), depth spec 252.5 -> 264.2/268.0 (+5.5%), clean same-session A/B post-cooldown. Two dq chains in flight per iteration + fused paired softmax update; +6 registers (the two-pass +32 collapsed occupancy — this is the frugal variant). V read from the V plane (correct values — unlike the retired kv twin).
gates: depth run-gen MATCH (4.902), short run-gen MATCH, VERIFY-GATE 0.000e0 K=4, stream 128/128
